description_long: This house features a modern, angular design with a combination of two main volumes joined at an offset, creating a dynamic and visually interesting structure. The exterior walls are a light gray, contrasted by a dark brown gable roof that covers both sections of the building. Bright green window frames and doors add a striking accent to the otherwise neutral palette, with multiple rectangular windows distributed asymmetrically across the facade. The main entryway is recessed and marked by a green door, sheltered by a small overhang. The house appears to have two stories, with the larger volume rising higher and the smaller section set back, providing architectural depth. The overall form is clean and geometric, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on functional, contemporary aesthetics.
description: A modern, two-story house with light gray walls, a dark brown gable roof, and bright green windows and doors. The structure features clean lines, asymmetrical window placement, and a recessed main entryway.
description_view_0: Front and left sides are visible, showing the main entryway, several green-framed windows, and the offset two-volume structure.
description_view_1: Rear view with the roof dominating the perspective; no windows or doors are visible, emphasizing the simple back wall.
description_view_2: Left side view highlights the stepped roofline and the smaller volume extending from the main structure, with minimal window visibility.
description_view_3: Right side and partial front view, showing the prominent gable end, green door, and a few windows, with the entryway partially visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed main entry with a green door, small overhang, and no porch or decorative columns.
